title: Earnings Call Transcript: Evgo's Q4 2024 Results Show Revenue Growth, Stock Dips
text: Evgo Inc reported its fourth-quarter and full-year 2024 financial results, demonstrating significant expansion in its charging network but falling short of earnings expectations. The electric vehicle charging infrastructure company posted revenues of $67.5 million in Q4, representing a 35% year-over-year increase. However, the company missed earnings per share forecasts, reporting a loss of $0.11 versus an anticipated loss of $0.09. For the full year 2024, Evgo achieved $257 million in revenue, a 60% increase from 2023. The company's charging network revenues more than doubled, driven by retail, commercial, and original equipment manufacturer segments. The company improved its adjusted EBITDA loss to $32.5 million annually, a $26.4 million improvement from the prior year. Following the earnings announcement, Evgo's stock declined 7.92% to $2.43 in post-market trading. Evgo added a record 1,200 operational stalls throughout 2024, reaching over 4,000 total stalls. Network utilization reached 24%, up from 19% the previous year. The company closed a $1.25 billion loan guarantee with the Department of Energy in December 2024. For 2025, Evgo projects revenues between $340-380 million and targets adjusted EBITDA breakeven.
